title: Creating with code
subject: Digital Solutions
description:
In Unit 1, students will explore the creative and technical aspects of developing interactive digital
solutions. They investigate algorithms, programming features and useability principles to
generate small interactive solutions using programming tools and gain a practical understanding
of programming features. This allows them the opportunity to explore existing and developing
trends involving digital technologies.
Unit objectives
1. Recognise and describe programming features and useability principles.
2. Symbolise and explain information, ideas and interrelationships related to digital problems.
3. Analyse problems and information related to a selected technology context.
4. Determine user experience and programming requirements, and success criteria of a digital
problem.
5. Synthesise information and ideas to develop possible prototype digital solutions.
6. Generate user interface and programmed components of the prototype digital solution.
7. Evaluate components and solutions against criteria to make refinements and justified
recommendations, and evaluate impacts.
8. Make decisions about and use mode-appropriate features, language and conventions for
particular purposes and contexts.
Technology contexts
Schools select a single technology context to examine problems in this unit. Schools may select
more than one programming language to cover the required operations to be performed.
